features.name: Speed
features.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

features.name: Draconic Ancestry
features.description: You have draconic ancestry of a particular type of dragon. Choose one type from the following: Black (Acid, 5×30 ft. line), Blue (Lightning, 5×30 ft. line), Brass (Fire, 5×30 ft. line), Bronze (Lightning, 5×30 ft. line), Copper (Acid, 5×30 ft. line), Gold (Fire, 15 ft. cone), Green (Poison, 15 ft. cone), Red (Fire, 15 ft. cone), Silver (Cold, 15 ft. cone), or White (Cold, 15 ft. cone). Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by your ancestry type.

features.name: Breath Weapon
features.description: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Damage Resistance
features.description: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

features.name: Languages
features.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

features.name: Retainers
features.description:
You have the service of three retainers loyal to your family. These retainers can be commoners who perform mundane tasks for you, but they do not fight for you, will not follow you into obviously dangerous situations, and will leave if they are frequently endangered or abused.

One of your retainers is replaced by a noble squire who serves you in pursuit of their own knighthood. The other retainers might include a groom to care for your horse and a servant who polishes your armor.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description:
You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. 
 Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

features.name: Action Surge
features.description: On your turn, you can take one additional action. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

features.name: Combat Superiority: Ambush
features.description: You learn maneuvers that are fueled by special dice called superiority dice. You learn three maneuvers of your choice. Many maneuvers enhance an attack in some way. You can use only one maneuver per attack. You learn two additional maneuvers of your choice at 7th, 10th, and 15th level. Each time you learn new maneuvers. You have four superiority dice, which are d8s. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain all of your expended superiority dice when you finish a short or long rest. You gain another superiority die at 7th level and one more at 15th level. Some of your maneuvers require your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects.

features.name: Combat Superiority: Bait and Switch

features.name: Student of War: Bait and Switch
Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice when you take the Attack action on your turn.

items.name: Chain Mail
items.description: Heavy armor. AC 16. Requires Strength 13. Disadvantage on Stealth checks.

items.name: Shield
items.description: Shield. +2 AC. Cannot be used with a two-handed weapon.
